Summary: As a user I can upload a PDF brief for processing
Description: As a paralegal or litigation support specialist, I need to upload a multi-page PDF brief so the system can scan it for exhibit citations.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Accept PDF files via drag-and-drop or file picker
- Display file name and size after upload
- Show error for non-PDF files
- Allow clearing/replacing the uploaded file
- Support files up to 50MB

Summary: As a user I can upload multiple exhibit files
Description: As a paralegal, I need to upload exhibit files (PDFs, images, Word docs) and label them so they can be linked to citations in the brief.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Accept multiple files at once via drag-and-drop or picker
- Auto-detect exhibit label from filename (e.g., Exhibit_A.pdf -> Exhibit A)
- Allow manual editing of exhibit labels
- Display list of uploaded exhibits with file names
- Support removing individual exhibits
- Support PDF, DOCX, JPG, PNG, TIFF file types

Summary: As a user I can scan a brief for exhibit citations
Description: As a paralegal, I need the system to automatically find all exhibit references in my uploaded brief so I know what needs to be linked.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Detect 'Exhibit A/1' format citations
- Detect 'Ex. A/1' abbreviated citations
- Detect 'Exh. A/1' abbreviated citations
- Detect compound references (Exhibits A and B)
- Detect citations with page references (Ex. A at 12)
- Detect citations with Bates number references
- Show total count of citations found
- Show count broken down by linked vs unlinked

Summary: As a user I can see citations auto-mapped to exhibits
Description: As a paralegal, I want the system to automatically match detected citations to my uploaded exhibits by label so I don't have to manually link each one.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Auto-map citations where exhibit label matches (Exhibit A -> file labeled Exhibit A)
- Show count of auto-mapped vs unlinked citations
- Display matched exhibit name next to each linked citation
- Handle case-insensitive matching
- Handle label variations (Exhibit A, Ex. A, Exh. A all map to same exhibit)

Summary: As a user I can manually map unlinked citations to exhibits
Description: As a paralegal, I need to manually assign exhibits to citations that weren't auto-matched, and override incorrect auto-mappings.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Show dropdown of available exhibits for each citation
- Allow clearing a mapping (unlinking)
- Show context snippet around each citation for verification
- Show page number where citation appears
- Changes reflected immediately in preview

Summary: As a user I can preview the brief with highlighted citations
Description: As a paralegal, I need to see a preview of the brief text with citations highlighted and color-coded so I can verify the linking before generating output.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Display full brief text in readable format
- Highlight linked citations in green
- Highlight unlinked citations in amber/warning color
- Clicking a linked citation opens the exhibit in a new tab
- Show legend for highlight colors

Summary: As a user I can download the linked brief as HTML
Description: As a paralegal, I need to download the brief with embedded hyperlinks so I can share it with attorneys and clients.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Generate HTML file with clickable exhibit links
- Preserve brief text formatting
- Links open exhibit files in new tab
- Include header showing file name and link count
- Professional styling suitable for client delivery
- Download triggers automatically with correct filename

Summary: As a user I can download the linked brief as PDF
Description: As a paralegal, I want to download a PDF version of the linked brief with embedded URI annotations pointing to exhibits.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Generate modified PDF with link annotations at citation locations
- Preserve original PDF formatting and layout
- Links are clickable in standard PDF viewers
- Fallback gracefully if PDF generation fails (offer HTML instead)

Summary: As a user I can start over and process another brief
Description: As a paralegal, I need to reset the tool and start fresh so I can process multiple briefs in succession.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Clear all uploaded files and state
- Return to upload step
- Free memory (revoke object URLs)
- No data persists between sessions

Summary: As a user I see a clear workflow with step indicators
Description: As a user, I want to see where I am in the process so I know what steps are remaining.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Show 4-step indicator: Upload > Process > Review > Download
- Highlight current step
- Show completed steps differently from upcoming steps
- Responsive on mobile

Summary: As a user I can toggle dark mode
Description: As a user who works long hours, I want a dark mode option to reduce eye strain.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Toggle between light and dark themes
- Apply Veritext brand colors in both modes
- Persist preference (optional for POC)

Summary: As an admin I can see processing statistics
Description: As a Veritext product manager, I want to see how many citations were found and linked so I can validate the tool's accuracy.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Show total citations found
- Show linked count and percentage
- Show unlinked count
- Display stats prominently in review step

Summary: As a user I get helpful error messages when something goes wrong
Description: As a paralegal, I need clear error messages if something fails so I know what to do next.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Show error banner with readable message
- Handle PDF parsing failures gracefully
- Handle empty/corrupt PDF files
- Don't lose uploaded data on recoverable errors

Summary: Performance: Brief processing completes in under 10 seconds
Description: As a user, I need the citation scanning to complete quickly so I'm not waiting around.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Process a 30-page brief in under 10 seconds
- Show loading indicator during processing
- No browser freezing during processing

Summary: Security: Uploaded files are processed client-side only
Description: As a law firm user handling confidential documents, I need assurance that brief and exhibit files never leave my browser.
Acceptance Criteria:
- All PDF parsing happens in browser (no server upload)
- All linking happens client-side
- No data sent to any external API
- Document content never logged or stored
